[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Floating damage numbers for Skyrim. NG with one DLL for [b]SE, AE, and VR[/b], with world-anchored 3D numbers in VR and a built-in combat analytics log. Fully open source.

In VR the numbers are real 3D billboards at the target's position, rendered through [url=https://github.com/alandtse/imgui-vr-helper]ImGuiVRHelper[/url]'s world-quad API with per-pixel depth occlusion[/color][/size]

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Install with MO2 or Vortex like any other SKSE plugin. One DLL serves all three runtimes. Configure via [i]Data/SKSE/Plugins/FloatingDamageNG.ini[/i] (fully commented) or in-game with SKSE Menu Framework installed.[/color][/size]

[size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]

[*][color=#D4D4D8][b]Numbers colored by cause:[/b] physical, fire, frost, shock, poison, untyped magic, and green +N healing. An enchanted-weapon swing shows two numbers (the physical hit and the enchant payload) because the engine applies them separately and so do we. Amounts come from the engine's damage and magic-effect pipelines (true per-application values with source and caster), not from polling health bars.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][b]Real hit detail:[/b] CRIT / SNEAK / BASH / BLOCK from the engine's actual hit flags, and a mitigation subtext that says what happened: 104 (-45 armor), (-30 blocked) with the true blocked amount, (-20 resisted) and sized by how much of the hit was mitigated.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][b]HEADSHOT tags[/b] on bow/crossbow hits, resolved from the engine's hit position against the victim's skeleton. Works in vanilla; stays accurate under locational-damage mods (an implied x2.5 shows when a hit exceeds its normal baseline).[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][b]Numbers follow their target:[/b] a fleeing enemy under Flames carries its counting-up burn total; your self-heal rides along as you move. DoT ticks pool into one live number instead of spam.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][b]Readable in crowds:[/b] rapid hits alternate sides instead of overlapping, and a selectable origin marker (colored outline, underline, box, or none) says whose fight it is (your hits black, damage you take red, follower damage blue, bystander fights gray; all themable with a live preview in the menu). Follower/NPC numbers render smaller and distance-culled.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][b]Customizable motion and size:[/b] an effect is a set of physics values (rise, gravity, launch speed, damping, spread pattern, per-hit angle); presets are saved values. Float is the default; Arc, Radial, Fireworks, and Freeze/Accelerate/Bound/Drop ship as editable JSON in Data/SKSE/Plugins/FloatingDamageNG/Presets, and the current effect saves back to a shareable file. Each damage type can take its own preset and font. Fonts are chosen in-game from a drop-in folder plus the Windows fonts, applied on restart. Sizing has a base, a multiplier, log-scaled growth for large hits, and distance controls (a flat shrink floor or a VR growth cap) so distant hits stay readable; optional 1.2k abbreviation and squash-and-stretch. Live preview spawns samples on a console-selected target (prid, else the player).[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][b]Combat analytics:[/b] every fight logged as a session. with per-combatant damage, healing, crits, time-to-die, who fled, real/active/peak DPS and with per-combatant drill-downs: damage by weapon/spell and by target as kind-colored meter bars, a resist profile per enemy, and a death recap (who landed the killing blow and the hits before it). Stats browser with DPS graphs, sortable tables, and a name filter; live DPS readout on flat and on a head-locked HUD plane in VR (off by default); plain-text log plus opt-in JSONL/CSV exports for pandas/Excel and all auto-rotated.[/color]


[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]SE/AE: SKSE64 + Address Library. VR: SKSE VR + VR Address Library + [url=https://github.com/alandtse/imgui-vr-helper]ImGuiVRHelper[/url] 1.5+ (without it, numbers stay off in VR rather than falling back to a flat overlay). [/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]Optional: [url=https://github.com/alandtse/SKSE-Menu-Framework-3]SKSE Menu Framework[/url] for the in-game settings panel (color pickers, sliders, live style preview) and the combat-stats browser (DPS graphs, sortable tables, drill-downs, death recaps). [/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]Optional: [url=https://github.com/alandtse/devbench]devbench[/url] - when installed and enabled (off by default), combat stats are queryable over local MCP/REST for external tooling.

Source and issue tracker on the [url=https://github.com/alandtse/FloatingDamageNG]GitHub repository[/url] - bug reports with your FloatingDamageNG.log (and crash log if applicable) are the most useful thing you can send.[/color]

[size=4][b]Shout outs[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8][url=https://github.com/powerof3]powerof3[/url] — the display architecture builds on Floating Subtitles and the hit-data capture on KillFeed. [/color]
[color=#D4D4D8][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/Felisky384]Felisky384[/url] — the original Floating Damage defined the genre. [/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]The [url=https://github.com/alandtse/CommonLibVR/tree/ng]CommonLibSSE-NG[/url] maintainers and contributors, whose work makes one-DLL-for-three-runtimes possible.[/color]

Want to support me or help me top off my AI credits? [url=https://buymeacoffee.com/alandtse]Buy Me a Coffee[/url]
